title: Stop and skip
why.untouched: script.js is exactly as it was.
hint2.untouched: The served count is right. Check the number checked — the coffee runs out at the twelfth, and the queue is walked to the fortieth.
why.not-linked: The script never reaches the page.
why.crashed: Your code stopped with an error.
hint2.crashed: Look at the Console panel below.
hint2.no-break: Once the coffee runs out there is no point walking the rest of the queue.
hint2.no-continue: The second condition is the negation of the first. There is a way to write only one of them.
hint2.wrong-checked: The loop walks all forty people, even though the coffee ran out at the twelfth.
hint3.wrong-checked: break stops the whole loop on the spot. Execution carries on after the closing brace.
hint4.wrong-checked: At the top of the body, check whether the coffee has run out, and if so — stop.
hint2.wrong-paying: Twenty people, every fifth one is free. So sixteen pay.
hint2.wrong-skipped: Every fifth of twenty is four.
why.wrong-count: There are not four lines in the console.
why.lost-p: A paragraph has disappeared from the page.
blocks.0.text: A queue of forty people, and coffee for twelve. Plus a promotion: every fifth person drinks free.
blocks.1.text: The code gives the right number served. But it walks all forty people, even though the coffee ran out at the twelfth. Twenty-eight checks for nothing.
blocks.3.text: When the end condition is not known in advance but depends on something inside the loop, you stop it from within.
blocks.5.text: break leaves the loop immediately. The remaining repetitions do not happen, and execution carries on after the closing brace.
blocks.7.text: Sometimes you do not want to stop, only to skip the current repetition.
blocks.9.text: continue ends just this turn and moves to the next. The lines below it do not run this time.
blocks.11.text: The code today writes one condition and then its negation. The two always change together — forget one and you get a case that falls into neither.
blocks.12.text: With continue you write only the exception, and the normal case stays below, with no nesting.
blocks.13.quote: Leave early on the exceptions and keep the usual case at the bottom, where it reads most easily.
blocks.15.text: Make the first loop stop as soon as the coffee runs out. Make the second use continue instead of the two opposite conditions.
blocks.16.text: Afterwards the checked count should be twelve, not forty.
